items.q: What did Magna Carta actually do in 1215?
items.correct: It was a peace treaty between King John and rebel barons protecting baronial privileges and limiting arbitrary royal taxation and imprisonment — it was annulled within months.
items.misconception: Magna Carta gave ordinary English people democratic rights and freedom.
items.random_wrong: Magna Carta abolished the monarchy and created Parliament.
items.why: Magna Carta protected the elite, not commoners; villeins are barely mentioned. Its democratic reputation is a later (17th-century) reinterpretation projected backwards.

items.q: Why did Chartism fail in the short term?
items.correct: Mass petitions were rejected by Parliament; the movement split between moral-force and physical-force wings; economic improvement after 1848 reduced support.
items.misconception: Chartism failed because no one supported it.
items.random_wrong: Chartism failed because Queen Victoria personally banned the movement.
items.why: Chartism had millions of signatures and was hugely supported. It failed politically, not for lack of support — and five of its six points were eventually adopted.

items.q: What caused the Peasants' Revolt of 1381?
items.correct: The poll tax of 1381 on top of long-term anger over labour laws restricting peasant wages after the Black Death and resentment at clerical wealth.
items.misconception: The peasants revolted because they were starving and had no food.
items.random_wrong: The peasants wanted to make Wat Tyler king of England.
items.why: Post-plague wages were actually rising, which is why landlords tried to cap them. The revolt was about freedom and tax, not famine — a counter-intuitive point students miss.

items.q: In what year was Magna Carta sealed?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 1215. “1066” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Who led the Peasants' Revolt of 1381?
items.why: It's Wat Tyler. “Simon de Montfort” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year was Charles I executed?
items.why: The real figure is 1649. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
